agency: NYC Department of Small Business Services (SBS)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/sbs (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace) and the MyCity Business portal at nyc-business.nyc.gov/nycbusiness (identified as a Spring-session Java app with Adobe AEM Universal Editor + jQuery/Handlebars, Akamai mPulse; the Step-by-Step licensing wizard confirmed at /nycbusiness/wizard, title 'Step by Step'). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Small Business Services (SBS)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 28 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: SBS is a split domain: an informational NYC.gov site (Akamai/nginx/Livesite/Dynatrace) plus a business service portal — MyCity Business (nyc-business.nyc.gov) — a stateful Spring/Adobe-AEM application with no API.

The program data is reasonably OPEN: 28 NYC Open Data datasets covering the SBS Certified Business List (M/WBE, 56 columns), Business Improvement Districts (directory, maps, and a 64-column FY24 trends report), Workforce1 recruitment events and job listings, service-center locations, and business-incentive rolls.

But SBS is a NAVIGATOR agency, and its navigation is locked: the Step-by-Step licensing wizard (which licenses/permits does my business need?), incentive eligibility, and the M/WBE certification and Workforce1 enrollment flows live only inside the session-bound MyCity Business portal — none has a machine-readable surface.
The public 'SBS Certified Business List' is the shadow of a closed flow: you can read who got certified, but there is no API to apply.
Net-new write surface: apply for M/WBE certification (MWBECertificationApplication) — the flagship SBS business transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 28 SBS datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open SBS API — program/directory data only.

MyCity Business — Step-by-Step licensing wizard (/nycbusiness/wizard), incentive/eligibility lookups, M/WBE certification and Workforce1 enrollment. Spring SESSION cookie, jQuery/Handlebars, JavaScript-only, no JSON/OpenAPI.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
loaded by MyCity Business (cors.js) for content authoring.

fruit.name: SBS Certified Businesses (M/WBE)
fruit.notes: The flagship SBS directory — M/WBE, EBE, LBE, EO50 certified vendors. Keyed on Account_Number; rich with NAICS industry, bonding/contract capacity, ownership ethnicity, and full geography spine. It is the public OUTPUT of the closed certification flow.

fruit.name: Business Improvement Districts (BIDs)
fruit.notes: Defined commercial districts funding supplemental services, overseen by SBS. Directory + boundary maps + a 64-column FY24 trends report (finances, sanitation, public safety, storefronts).

fruit.name: Workforce1 Recruitment Events
fruit.notes: SBS hiring events connecting jobseekers to employers; title, employer/type, job family, qualifications, date, check-in window, borough.

fruit.name: Workforce1 Job Listings
fruit.notes: Open positions posted through Workforce1; title, sector/subsector, SOC occupation, wage and hours ranges, education, borough flags.

fruit.name: Center & Service Locations
fruit.notes: NYC Business Solutions Centers and Workforce1 Career Centers — the in-person storefronts; type, hours, details, address, geography, coordinates.

fruit.name: Business Incentives
opendata_match: Value of Energy Cost Savings Program bug8-9f3g (30c)
fruit.notes: Records of businesses that received SBS-administered benefits — energy cost savings, ICAP abatement, acceleration. Modeled as one entity keyed by program; carries savings, jobs created/retained, industry, geography.

fruit.name: Step-by-Step licensing wizard
fruit.notes: The Step-by-Step wizard tells a business which licenses and permits it needs to open. Pure session state in MyCity Business (title 'Step by Step'); no API. The guidance engine that is SBS's core value has no machine-readable surface.

fruit.name: Workforce1 enrollment
fruit.notes: Enrolling as a jobseeker with Workforce1 — MyCity-portal-only; no API. An alternative net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Apply for M/WBE certification
fruit.notes: The flagship SBS business transaction — apply for M/WBE / EBE / LBE certification. Lives only in the MyCity Business portal / PASSPort; no API and no Open Data twin (only the approved OUTPUT is public in ci93-uc8s). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: ICAP Closeout Contract Amount Spreadsheet
fruit.notes: ICAP closeout compliance-reporting workbook; a structured form that should be a submission API.

fruit.name: Sample District Needs Survey
fruit.notes: Sample commercial-district needs-survey workbook for BID formation.

meta.platform: Informational site on the NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); the transactional MyCity Business portal on Spring (Java) + Adobe AEM + jQuery/Handlebars, hosting the Step-by-Step licensing wizard and certification/enrollment flows.
meta.netnew: MWBECertificationApplication — apply for M/WBE (also EBE/LBE) certification; no API today, only the MyCity Business portal.
meta.tagline: SBS publishes who got certified and where to hire, but hides the navigation — the wizard, eligibility, and certification that are the agency's real job.
meta.headline: SBS's program directories are open, but its guidance and certification engine is locked inside a stateful MyCity Business portal with no API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite site plus a real, stateful MyCity Business portal (Spring + Adobe AEM) — modern-ish but session-bound and vendor-authored.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A real transactional portal exists, but it exposes no API, OpenAPI, or JSON — only a session-walled UI.
scorecard_notes.openData: 28 rich SBS datasets on Socrata SODA (certified list 56c, BID trends 64c) — broad and well-columned.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Reference/directory objects are open, but the core transactions (certification application, enrollment, licensing guidance) have no data twin — only the approved output.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere; certification, enrollment, and the Step-by-Step wizard are portal-only.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no machine-readable guidance/eligibility.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Data lives on NYC's own Socrata; the portal is a city-run MyCity app (not a pure vendor CRM) but leans on Adobe AEM and session state.
